chunks.title: community — Our Community
chunks.content: HIRO is more than programs and products — it's a community of adults 30+ who are committed to becoming their best selves. We believe transformation happens better together.

chunks.title: community — Real Over Perfect
chunks.content: Share the struggles, not just the wins. Real progress is messy and non-linear. The community values honesty about the journey.

chunks.title: community — Support Over Judgment
chunks.content: Everyone is at a different stage. Some members are just starting. Others have been at this for years. All are welcome. All are respected.

chunks.title: community — Evidence Over Hype
chunks.content: We value what works over what's trendy. If someone shares advice, it should be grounded in evidence or experience — not bro science or Instagram trends.

chunks.title: community — Consistency Over Intensity
chunks.content: We celebrate daily discipline, not occasional heroics. The member who shows up 3x/week for a year has our respect more than the one who crushes a 30-day challenge and disappears.

chunks.title: community — Discussion Areas
chunks.content:
- **Daily Wins:** Share your small victories — the workout you almost skipped, the healthy choice you made, the progress you noticed
- **Questions & Advice:** Ask anything. Members and HIRO coaches respond with practical, experience-backed answers
- **Progress Journals:** Document your journey. The most inspiring content in HIRO is real members sharing real progress
- **Product & Program Feedback:** Honest reviews and experiences with HIRO products and programs

chunks.title: community — Accountability
chunks.content:
- Find an accountability partner with similar goals and schedule
- Weekly check-in threads help you stay on track
- Members often form small groups (3-5 people) for closer support

chunks.title: community — Events and Challenges
chunks.content:
- Monthly community challenges (step goals, consistency streaks, hydration)
- Live Q&A sessions with HIRO coaches
- Member spotlights celebrating outstanding progress

chunks.title: community — Community Guidelines
chunks.content:
1. Be respectful. Disagreement is fine. Disrespect is not.
2. No unsolicited selling or promotion of non-HIRO products
3. Medical advice: share experiences, not prescriptions. "This worked for me" is fine. "You should take this drug" is not.
4. Keep progress photos tasteful and focused on the transformation
5. What's shared in HIRO stays in HIRO — respect member privacy
6. Report concerns to community moderators, don't engage with trolls

chunks.title: community — If You're New
chunks.content:
- Introduce yourself in the welcome thread
- Share your goals and what brought you to HIRO
- Don't be shy about asking questions — everyone started somewhere
- Find 2-3 members whose journey resonates and follow their progress

chunks.title: community — If You're Experienced
chunks.content:
- Your experience is valuable — share what you've learned
- Encourage newer members — you were in their shoes once
- Be specific in your advice ("I did X and it helped with Y" not "just work harder")
- Remember that what worked for you may not work for everyone

chunks.title: community — The HIRO Difference
chunks.content:
What makes the HIRO community different:
- **Age-appropriate:** Everyone here is 30+. The challenges, solutions, and conversations are relevant to your stage of life.
- **No toxicity:** No body shaming, no extreme diet culture, no "no days off" mentality. We support sustainable progress.
- **Multi-dimensional:** This isn't just a fitness community. Members discuss skincare, career, relationships, confidence — the full picture.
- **Action-oriented:** We're here to do the work, not just talk about it. The community inspires action, not just discussion.

chunks.title: faq — What is HIRO?
chunks.content: HIRO is a premium glow-up and self-improvement community for busy adults 30+. We help members transform through fitness, nutrition, skincare, haircare, grooming, and lifestyle optimization — all built around realistic, sustainable approaches that fit into demanding lives.

chunks.title: faq — Who is HIRO for?
chunks.content: HIRO is for anyone 30+ who has spent years prioritizing career, family, and responsibilities and now wants to invest in themselves. If you want to improve how you look, feel, and perform — without extreme diets or unsustainable routines — HIRO is for you.

chunks.title: faq — Is HIRO for men or women?
chunks.content: HIRO is for everyone. Our approach works across all genders. The principles of fitness, nutrition, skincare, and lifestyle optimization are universal — we tailor the specifics to the individual.

chunks.title: faq — How is HIRO different from other fitness programs?
chunks.content: Most programs assume you have unlimited time, no responsibilities, and a 25-year-old's recovery capacity. HIRO is built for real adults with jobs, families, and busy schedules. We prioritize sustainability and integration into real life over extreme transformations.

chunks.title: faq — What programs does HIRO offer?
chunks.content: HIRO offers structured transformation programs covering fitness, nutrition, skincare, and lifestyle. Each program includes step-by-step guidance, video demonstrations, progress tracking, and community support.

chunks.title: faq — How long are the programs?
chunks.content: Programs vary from 4 weeks (kickstart) to 12 weeks (comprehensive transformation). We recommend starting with a 12-week program for meaningful results.

chunks.title: faq — Can I do the programs at home?
chunks.content: Yes. Most HIRO programs require minimal equipment and can be done at home, in a gym, or while traveling.

chunks.title: faq — What products does HIRO sell?
chunks.content: HIRO offers premium skincare, haircare, and wellness products formulated specifically for adults 30+. Every product is evidence-based, with ingredients backed by research, not marketing hype.

chunks.title: faq — Are HIRO products natural?
chunks.content: HIRO products use both natural and scientifically-proven synthetic ingredients. We prioritize efficacy and safety over "natural" marketing claims. Every ingredient is chosen because it works, not because it sounds good on a label.

chunks.title: faq — Where are HIRO products made?
chunks.content: All HIRO products are manufactured in-house at our own facility, giving us complete control over quality, formulation, and consistency. We don't white-label — we create.

chunks.title: faq — How long until I see results?
chunks.content: Most members notice improvements in energy and confidence within 2 weeks. Visible physical changes typically appear at 4-8 weeks, with significant transformation by 12 weeks. Consistency is the key variable.

chunks.title: faq — What if I'm not seeing results?
chunks.content: First: are you tracking the right things? Weight alone doesn't tell the story — measurements, photos, energy levels, and how clothes fit matter more. Second: nutrition typically needs attention before training. Third: sleep and stress can completely block progress.

chunks.title: faq — Is this a quick fix?
chunks.content: No. HIRO is for lasting transformation. Quick fixes fail 95% of the time. We teach sustainable systems that create permanent change.

chunks.title: faq — How does the community work?
chunks.content: HIRO members connect through our platform, sharing progress, asking questions, and supporting each other. We believe accountability and community are powerful drivers of success.

chunks.title: faq — Can I interact with other members?
chunks.content: Yes. The community is a core part of HIRO. Members share wins, discuss challenges, and learn from each other's experiences.

chunks.title: faq — How much does HIRO cost?
chunks.content: HIRO membership is $25.99/month. This includes access to all programs, the community, and ongoing content. Products are purchased separately.

chunks.title: faq — Can I cancel anytime?
chunks.content: Yes. There are no long-term contracts. You can cancel your membership at any time and it will not renew.

chunks.title: faq — Is there a free trial?
chunks.content: We don't offer free trials because our approach requires commitment to see results. However, you can cancel after the first month if it's not right for you.

chunks.title: faq — How do I access HIRO?
chunks.content: HIRO is accessible through our website at hiroprotocol.com. All programs, community features, and content are available through the web platform.

chunks.title: faq — Can I use HIRO on my phone?
chunks.content: Yes, the HIRO platform is fully mobile-responsive and works on any device.

chunks.title: faq — How do I contact support?
chunks.content: Reach out through the HIRO platform or email support. We respond within 24 hours on business days.

chunks.title: fitness — Philosophy
chunks.content: Fitness at HIRO is about sustainable, practical improvement — not extreme transformation. Members are busy adults 30+ balancing careers, families, and responsibilities. The goal is consistent progress that fits into real life.

chunks.title: fitness — Consistency Over Intensity
chunks.content:
- 3-4 workouts per week is ideal for most members
- 30-45 minute sessions are effective and manageable
- Something is always better than nothing — a 15-minute walk counts

chunks.title: fitness — Movement Quality First
chunks.content:
- Proper form prevents injury and builds lasting results
- Master bodyweight movements before adding weight
- Mobility work is not optional — it's essential after 30

chunks.title: fitness — Progressive Overload
chunks.content:
- Small weekly increases in weight, reps, or time
- Track workouts to ensure progress
- Deload every 6-8 weeks to prevent burnout

chunks.title: fitness — Busy Professional (3x/week)
chunks.content:
- Full body, each session
- Focus on compound movements
- 45 minutes per session

chunks.title: fitness — Balanced Schedule (4x/week)
chunks.content:
- Upper/Lower split
- 2 upper, 2 lower days
- 35-40 minutes per session

chunks.title: fitness — Time-Efficient (2x/week)
chunks.content:
- Full body both days
- Superset format to save time
- Focus on quality over quantity

chunks.title: fitness — Foundation Movements
chunks.content:
- Goblet squats (easier on back than barbell)
- Push-ups and variations
- Dumbbell rows
- Romanian deadlifts
- Walking lunges
- Pallof press (core stability)

chunks.title: fitness — Mobility Essentials
chunks.content:
- Hip flexor stretches
- Thoracic spine rotations
- Shoulder dislocates with band
- Ankle mobility drills
- Cat-cow flow

chunks.title: fitness — "I don't have time"
chunks.content:
- Morning workouts before the day starts
- 20-minute high-density sessions
- Lunch break movement breaks
- Walking meetings

chunks.title: fitness — "I'm too tired after work"
chunks.content:
- Morning routine is better for consistency
- Pre-workout nutrition matters
- Sleep quality directly impacts energy
- Start with 2x/week and build up

chunks.title: fitness — "I don't know what I'm doing"
chunks.content:
- HIRO provides structured programs
- Start with bodyweight foundations
- Video form checks available
- Community support for questions

chunks.title: fitness — "I'm not seeing results"
chunks.content:
- Nutrition is 70% of body composition
- Track measurements, not just weight
- Progress photos tell the real story
- 8-12 weeks for visible changes

chunks.title: fitness — Sleep
chunks.content:
- 7-8 hours is non-negotiable
- Consistent sleep/wake times
- Cool, dark room for optimal sleep

chunks.title: fitness — Nutrition for Recovery
chunks.content:
- Protein within 2 hours of training
- Hydration throughout the day
- Anti-inflammatory foods (berries, fatty fish, turmeric)

chunks.title: fitness — Active Recovery
chunks.content:
- Daily walks (8-10k steps)
- Light stretching on off days
- Foam rolling 2-3x/week

chunks.title: fitness — Age-Specific Considerations (30+)
chunks.content:
- Longer warm-ups needed (8-10 minutes)
- Joint health becomes a priority
- Recovery takes longer than in 20s
- Testosterone-supporting practices matter
- HIIT should be limited to 1-2x/week

chunks.title: haircare — Philosophy
chunks.content: Hair health is part of the complete transformation picture. Whether the goal is maintaining healthy hair, addressing thinning, or optimizing growth, HIRO takes an evidence-based approach without gimmicks or false promises.

chunks.title: haircare — Scalp Health is Hair Health
chunks.content:
- Healthy hair starts at the scalp
- Regular cleansing prevents buildup that clogs follicles
- Scalp massage increases blood flow
- Treat scalp issues (dandruff, inflammation) promptly

chunks.title: haircare — Gentle Handling
chunks.content:
- Wet hair is fragile — be gentle when towel-drying
- Wide-tooth comb for detangling, not a brush
- Avoid tight hairstyles that cause tension
- Heat protection is non-negotiable before styling

chunks.title: haircare — Nutrition Matters
chunks.content:
- Hair is non-essential tissue — the body prioritizes it last
- Protein deficiency shows in hair first
- Iron, zinc, vitamin D, and biotin support hair health
- Crash diets cause temporary hair shedding

chunks.title: haircare — First-Line Approaches
chunks.content:
- Minoxidil (topical): FDA-approved, effective for crown and diffuse thinning
- Finasteride (oral, prescription): blocks DHT, effective but requires doctor
- Ketoconazole shampoo (Nizoral): mild anti-androgen effect
- Start early — prevention is easier than regrowth

chunks.title: haircare — Supportive Practices
chunks.content:
- Scalp massage 5-10 minutes daily (increases blood flow)
- Microneedling (dermaroller) 1x/week with 1.5mm (use with minoxidil for better results)
- Reduce scalp inflammation with gentle products
- Avoid harsh chemical treatments

chunks.title: haircare — What Doesn't Work
chunks.content:
- Biotin supplements (unless deficient — rare)
- Caffeine shampoos (no meaningful evidence)
- "Natural" DHT blockers (saw palmetto has very weak evidence)
- Special pillows or laser combs (weak evidence for most)

chunks.title: haircare — Hair Loss and Thinning (Female Pattern)
chunks.content:
- Diffuse thinning rather than receding hairline
- Often related to hormonal changes, stress, or nutritional gaps
- Minoxidil is first-line for women too (2% or 5%)
- Iron and vitamin D levels should be checked
- Gentle styling — avoid tension and excessive heat

chunks.title: haircare — Washing Frequency
chunks.content:
- Oily scalp: daily or every other day
- Normal scalp: every 2-3 days
- Dry/curly hair: 1-2x per week
- Use dry shampoo between washes sparingly

chunks.title: haircare — Product Selection
chunks.content:
- Sulfate-free shampoos are gentler
- Silicone-free conditioners for fine hair
- Protein treatments for damaged hair
- Leave-in conditioner for dry or curly hair

chunks.title: haircare — Styling
chunks.content:
- Heat protectant always before hot tools
- Lowest effective heat setting
- Air dry when possible
- Silk/satin pillowcase reduces friction and breakage

chunks.title: haircare — Stress
chunks.content:
- Telogen effluvium: stress-induced shedding (temporary, 3-6 months)
- Chronic stress impacts hair growth cycle
- Stress management supports hair health

chunks.title: haircare — Sleep
chunks.content:
- Growth hormone released during deep sleep
- 7-8 hours for optimal recovery
- Silk pillowcase protects hair overnight

chunks.title: haircare — Exercise
chunks.content:
- Improves circulation including to scalp
- Reduces stress (lowers cortisol)
- Sweat can irritate scalp — rinse after intense workouts

chunks.title: haircare — When to See a Specialist
chunks.content:
- Sudden patchy hair loss
- Scarring or redness on scalp
- Hair loss with other symptoms (fatigue, weight changes)
- No response to over-the-counter treatments after 6 months
- Consider a dermatologist who specializes in hair disorders

chunks.title: lifestyle — Philosophy
chunks.content: True transformation goes beyond fitness and nutrition. HIRO helps members optimize all aspects of their lifestyle — sleep, stress, confidence, grooming, and daily routines — to create lasting change.

chunks.title: lifestyle — Why Sleep Matters
chunks.content:
- Recovery happens during sleep (muscle repair, hormone production)
- Poor sleep increases cortisol (stress hormone) and hunger hormones
- Decision-making and willpower decline with sleep deficit
- Consistent poor sleep undermines every other health effort

chunks.title: lifestyle — The HIRO Sleep Protocol
chunks.content:
1. Consistent wake time (even on weekends)
2. Morning sunlight exposure (10-15 minutes within 30 min of waking)
3. No caffeine after 2 PM
4. Screen curfew: 60 minutes before bed (or blue-light blocking glasses)
5. Cool room: 65-68°F (18-20°C)
6. Dark room: blackout curtains or sleep mask
7. Wind-down routine: reading, light stretching, meditation

chunks.title: lifestyle — Common Sleep Disruptors
chunks.content:
- Alcohol: helps fall asleep but destroys sleep quality
- Late meals: digestion interferes with deep sleep
- Inconsistent schedule: circadian rhythm needs regularity
- Phone in bedroom: temptation and blue light
- Caffeine sensitivity increases with age

chunks.title: lifestyle — Understanding Stress
chunks.content:
- Acute stress is normal and adaptive
- Chronic stress is the enemy of transformation
- Cortisol promotes fat storage (especially abdominal)
- Stress impairs recovery, sleep, and decision-making

chunks.title: lifestyle — Practical Stress Tools
chunks.content:
- Morning routine (even 10 minutes of quiet time)
- Breathwork: 4-7-8 breathing (inhale 4s, hold 7s, exhale 8s)
- Daily walk without phone/podcasts (mental reset)
- Journaling: 5 minutes of brain dump before bed
- Setting boundaries: learning to say no
- Professional support when needed (therapy is maintenance, not crisis-only)

chunks.title: lifestyle — Work-Life Balance (For Busy Professionals)
chunks.content:
- Define non-negotiable personal time
- Batch similar tasks to reduce context switching
- Take actual lunch breaks away from desk
- Protect workout time like a meeting
- Delegate when possible — at work and at home

chunks.title: lifestyle — Daily Grooming Baseline
chunks.content:
- Clean, well-moisturized skin
- Maintained facial hair (clean shaven or well-groomed beard)
- Clean, trimmed nails
- Fresh, appropriate fragrance (less is more)
- Well-fitted clothes make more impact than expensive clothes

chunks.title: lifestyle — Elevating Your Presentation
chunks.content:
- Find a consistent haircut schedule (every 3-4 weeks)
- Invest in quality basics: well-fitting jeans, clean white shirt, good shoes
- Posture communicates confidence before you speak
- Eye contact and a genuine smile are free and powerful
- Dress for how you want to feel, not just the occasion

chunks.title: lifestyle — Internal Work
chunks.content:
- Confidence comes from keeping promises to yourself
- Set and achieve small daily goals (momentum)
- Track wins, not just gaps
- Comparison is the thief of progress — compare to your past self
- Competence breeds confidence — invest in skills

chunks.title: lifestyle — External Practices
chunks.content:
- Posture: shoulders back, head up
- Speak slightly slower than feels natural
- Prepare for important conversations and meetings
- Dress intentionally for confidence-boosting situations
- Physical fitness directly impacts self-perception

chunks.title: lifestyle — The HIRO Morning (30-45 minutes)
chunks.content:
1. Wake at consistent time
2. Hydrate: 500ml water immediately
3. Sunlight exposure: 10 minutes outside or by window
4. Movement: walk, stretch, or workout
5. Mindfulness: 5 minutes meditation or journaling
6. Protein-rich breakfast
7. Review top 3 priorities for the day

chunks.title: lifestyle — Evening Wind-Down
chunks.content:
1. Last meal 2-3 hours before bed
2. Dim lights after sunset
3. Screen-free activity (reading, conversation, hobby)
4. Prepare for tomorrow (clothes, meals, schedule)
5. Gratitude: note 3 things that went well today
6. Consistent bedtime

chunks.title: lifestyle — Relationships and Social Health
chunks.content:
- Strong relationships are a top predictor of happiness and longevity
- Schedule quality time with important people
- Be present: phone away during conversations
- Men especially benefit from maintaining close friendships
- Community (like HIRO) provides accountability and support

chunks.title: nutrition — Philosophy
chunks.content: Nutrition at HIRO is about fueling your best self without obsession. No extreme diets, no demonizing foods, no guilt. Just practical, evidence-based strategies that work for busy adults.

chunks.title: nutrition — Protein Priority
chunks.content:
- Aim for 1.6-2.0g per kg of bodyweight daily
- Distribute protein across 3-4 meals
- Prioritize whole food sources: lean meats, fish, eggs, dairy, legumes
- Protein supports muscle maintenance, especially important after 30

chunks.title: nutrition — Whole Food Foundation
chunks.content:
- 80% of diet from minimally processed foods
- Colorful vegetables at most meals
- Quality carbohydrates for energy
- Healthy fats for hormone function

chunks.title: nutrition — Flexible Approach
chunks.content:
- 20% flexibility for social events, treats, dining out
- No forbidden foods
- Focus on what to add, not what to remove
- Sustainability over perfection

chunks.title: nutrition — Breakfast (within 2 hours of waking)
chunks.content:
- Protein + complex carb + fruit/vegetable
- Example: Greek yogurt with berries and oats
- Example: Eggs with whole grain toast and avocado

chunks.title: nutrition — Lunch
chunks.content:
- Lean protein + vegetables + quality carb
- Example: Grilled chicken salad with quinoa
- Example: Salmon with roasted vegetables

chunks.title: nutrition — Dinner
chunks.content:
- Protein + vegetables + optional carb based on activity level
- Example: Lean beef stir-fry with brown rice
- Example: White fish with sweet potato and greens

chunks.title: nutrition — Snacks (as needed)
chunks.content:
- Protein-focused: cottage cheese, hard-boiled eggs, protein shake
- Energy-focused: apple with almond butter, trail mix
- Recovery-focused: chocolate milk, protein bar

chunks.title: nutrition — Hydration
chunks.content:
- Minimum 2-3 liters of water daily
- More with exercise or hot weather
- Start morning with 500ml water
- Herbal teas count toward hydration
- Limit alcohol — it impacts sleep, recovery, and progress

chunks.title: nutrition — Recommended
chunks.content:
- Vitamin D3 (especially if low sun exposure)
- Omega-3 fatty acids (fish oil or algae)
- Magnesium (glycinate form for sleep)
- Creatine monohydrate (5g daily, benefits beyond muscle)
- Protein powder (convenience, not necessity)

chunks.title: nutrition — Not Recommended
chunks.content:
- Fat burners (ineffective, often dangerous)
- Testosterone boosters (no evidence for OTC products)
- BCAAs (whole protein is better)
- Detox teas (marketing, not science)

chunks.title: nutrition — Restaurant Guidelines
chunks.content:
- Protein + vegetables as the base of your order
- Ask for dressings and sauces on the side
- Don't fear asking for modifications
- Enjoy the meal — one dinner won't undo progress

chunks.title: nutrition — Social Events
chunks.content:
- Eat normally before the event
- Choose what you truly want, skip what you don't
- Alcohol: 1-2 drinks max, water between each
- Return to normal eating next meal — no compensation needed

chunks.title: nutrition — "I eat well but can't lose weight"
chunks.content:
- Track portions for 1-2 weeks to identify blind spots
- Liquid calories add up (coffee drinks, alcohol, juices)
- Sleep and stress impact fat loss significantly
- Consider a modest calorie deficit (300-500 calories)

chunks.title: nutrition — "I'm always hungry"
chunks.content:
- Increase protein at each meal
- Add more fiber (vegetables, legumes, whole grains)
- Check hydration — thirst mimics hunger
- Eat more volume foods (vegetables, berries, soups)

chunks.title: nutrition — "Meal prep takes too long"
chunks.content:
- Batch cook proteins for 3-4 days
- Pre-cut vegetables for quick assembly
- Keep healthy convenience options (rotisserie chicken, frozen vegetables, canned beans)
- 15-minute meals are completely viable

chunks.title: products — Product Philosophy
chunks.content:
Every HIRO product is formulated in-house for adults 30+. No white-label, no marketing gimmicks — just evidence-based ingredients at effective concentrations. We manufacture everything ourselves to ensure quality and consistency.

---

chunks.title: products — H01 — The Foundation Serum
chunks.content:
**Product Name:** H01 Foundation Serum
**Description:** Advanced multi-peptide and antioxidant serum designed for daily use. Supports collagen production, reduces fine lines, and protects against environmental damage.
**Benefits:**
- Stimulates collagen production for firmer skin
- Reduces appearance of fine lines and wrinkles
- Provides antioxidant protection against pollution and UV damage
- Improves skin texture and tone
- Lightweight, fast-absorbing formula

**Ingredients:**
- Matrixyl 3000 (palmitoyl tripeptide-1, palmitoyl tetrapeptide-7)
- Vitamin C (L-ascorbic acid, 15%)
- Niacinamide (vitamin B3, 5%)
- Hyaluronic acid (multi-weight for deep hydration)
- Vitamin E (tocopherol)
- Ferulic acid

**Who it helps:** Anyone concerned with visible signs of aging, dull skin, or uneven texture. Ideal for first-time serum users and experienced skincare enthusiasts alike.

**When to recommend:** When a member mentions fine lines, dullness, uneven skin tone, or wants to start an anti-aging routine. Also recommended as the foundation of any HIRO skincare stack.

**How to use:** Apply 3-4 drops to clean, dry skin every morning. Follow with moisturizer and SPF. Can be used morning and evening for enhanced results.

**Price:** $44.00
**Product URL:** https://hiroprotocol.com/products/h01-foundation-serum

---

chunks.title: products — H02 — Recovery Moisturizer
chunks.content:
**Product Name:** H02 Recovery Moisturizer
**Description:** Ceramide-rich overnight recovery moisturizer that repairs skin barrier while you sleep. Formulated with skin-identical lipids and peptides for maximum overnight regeneration.
**Benefits:**
- Repairs compromised skin barrier
- Deep hydration without greasiness
- Supports overnight skin regeneration
- Reduces morning puffiness and dullness
- Suitable for all skin types

**Ingredients:**
- Ceramides (AP, NP, EOP)
- Peptide complex
- Squalane
- Shea butter
- Niacinamide
- Panthenol (vitamin B5)
- Cholesterol

**Who it helps:** Anyone with dry, dehydrated, or stressed skin. Excellent for those using active ingredients (retinoids, exfoliants) that may compromise the skin barrier. Also ideal for those in dry climates or with indoor heating/AC exposure.

**When to recommend:** When a member complains of dryness, tightness, irritation from actives, or wants a richer night cream. Pairs perfectly with H01 as a day/night system.

**How to use:** Apply a pea-sized amount to face and neck as the last step of your evening routine. Use nightly for best results.

**Price:** $44.00
**Product URL:** https://hiroprotocol.com/products/h02-recovery-moisturizer

---

chunks.title: products — H03 — Renewal Scalp Treatment
chunks.content:
**Product Name:** H03 Renewal Scalp Treatment
**Description:** Daily-use scalp serum formulated to support healthy hair growth by optimizing scalp health. Contains clinically-studied ingredients that target follicle health and reduce scalp inflammation.
**Benefits:**
- Supports healthy hair growth cycle
- Reduces scalp inflammation and irritation
- Improves follicle health and function
- Lightweight, non-greasy formula
- Can be used alongside other hair loss treatments

**Ingredients:**
- Redensyl (targets hair follicle stem cells)
- Capixyl (peptide + red clover extract)
- Caffeine
- Biotin
- Niacinamide
- Rosemary extract
- Peppermint oil

**Who it helps:** Anyone experiencing early signs of hair thinning or wanting to proactively support hair health. Suitable for both men and women. Works well alongside minoxidil or other prescribed treatments.

**When to recommend:** When a member expresses concern about hair thinning, shedding, or wants to optimize hair health. Not a replacement for medical treatment of advanced hair loss — recommend seeing a dermatologist for significant loss.

**How to use:** Apply 1ml (one dropper) directly to scalp, focusing on areas of concern. Massage in gently. Use once daily, ideally after showering on a clean scalp.

**Price:** $44.00
**Product URL:** https://hiroprotocol.com/products/h03-renewal-scalp-treatment

chunks.title: products — Morning
chunks.content:
1. Cleanse
2. H01 Foundation Serum
3. Moisturizer
4. SPF

chunks.title: products — Evening
chunks.content:
1. Double cleanse
2. H01 Foundation Serum (optional PM use)
3. H02 Recovery Moisturizer
4. H03 Renewal Scalp Treatment (on clean scalp)

chunks.title: products — Product Compatibility
chunks.content: All HIRO products are formulated to work together. There are no conflicts or contraindications between any HIRO products. If using prescription treatments, consult your dermatologist about layering order.

chunks.title: programs — Program Philosophy
chunks.content:
Every HIRO program is designed for real adults with demanding lives. No 6 AM mandatory check-ins. No 2-hour workouts. No unsustainable restrictions. Just structured, progressive plans that deliver results while fitting into your actual schedule.

---

chunks.title: programs — The Foundation (12 Weeks)
chunks.content:
**Description:** Our flagship comprehensive transformation program. Everything you need to transform your body, skin, and habits over 12 weeks.

**What's included:**
- 3x/week progressive strength training program (30-45 min)
- Full nutrition framework with meal templates and recipes
- Skincare routine builder
- Daily habit tracking and accountability
- Weekly check-ins and progress assessments
- Community support group

**Who it's for:** Members ready for complete transformation. Best for those who can commit to consistent effort over 12 weeks and want to build permanent habits.

**Equipment needed:** Dumbbells or resistance bands, or gym access

**Expected results:** Visible body composition changes, significant strength improvements, established skincare routine, consistent sleep and nutrition habits.

---

chunks.title: programs — The Kickstart (4 Weeks)
chunks.content:
**Description:** A high-focus 4-week sprint to build momentum and establish the core HIRO habits. Perfect for getting started or resetting after a break.

**What's included:**
- 3x/week full-body workouts (25-35 min)
- Simple nutrition guidelines (no tracking required)
- Basic skincare routine
- Morning and evening habit checklist
- Daily motivation prompts

**Who it's for:** New members wanting a clear starting point. Experienced members returning after time off. Anyone who wants to prove to themselves they can be consistent for a month.

**Equipment needed:** Bodyweight only or minimal equipment

**Expected results:** 4-8 weeks of consistent habit formation, initial strength gains, improved energy and sleep, confidence from completing the commitment.

---

chunks.title: programs — The Executive (Ongoing)
chunks.content:
**Description:** Designed for the busiest professionals — maximum results in minimum time. Efficient workouts, streamlined nutrition, and lifestyle optimization for those who can't spare a minute.

**What's included:**
- 2-3x/week hyper-efficient workouts (20-30 min)
- Grab-and-go nutrition strategies
- Sleep optimization protocol
- Stress management tools
- Travel-friendly routine adaptations
- Meeting-friendly movement breaks

**Who it's for:** CEOs, executives, entrepreneurs, parents of young children, frequent travelers, anyone working 60+ hours/week.

**Equipment needed:** Minimal (bodyweight, bands) with hotel-room-friendly options

**Expected results:** Maintained fitness levels despite schedule, improved energy management, better sleep quality, stress reduction, consistent health habits integrated into demanding lifestyle.

---

chunks.title: programs — The Reset (6 Weeks)
chunks.content:
**Description:** A restorative program focused on recovery, mobility, and rebuilding fundamentals. Ideal after injury, burnout, or extended time away from fitness.

**What's included:**
- 3x/week mobility and functional movement sessions
- Anti-inflammatory nutrition protocol
- Sleep and recovery optimization
- Stress reduction practices
- Progressive return to strength work (weeks 4-6)

**Who it's for:** Members recovering from injury or illness. Those experiencing burnout or chronic fatigue. Anyone who has been sedentary and wants a safe return to activity.

**Equipment needed:** Foam roller, resistance bands, optional light dumbbells

**Expected results:** Improved mobility and movement quality, reduced pain and stiffness, restored energy levels, safe foundation for more intense training.

---

chunks.title: programs — The Skin Protocol (8 Weeks)
chunks.content:
**Description:** A dedicated skincare transformation program that builds a complete, evidence-based routine for your specific skin type and concerns.

**What's included:**
- Personalized skincare routine builder
- Ingredient education (what works and why)
- Weekly routine adjustments based on skin response
- Nutrition for skin health guide
- Lifestyle factors that impact skin
- Product recommendations (HIRO and beyond)

**Who it's for:** Anyone wanting to transform their skin but feeling overwhelmed by options. Those who've tried products without results. Members wanting to age well and maintain healthy skin long-term.

**Expected results:** Clearer, more even skin tone, reduced fine lines and texture, established sustainable routine, confidence in choosing and using products.

---

chunks.title: programs — Program Selection Guide
chunks.content:
**I want to lose weight and get in shape →** The Foundation

**I'm completely new to fitness →** The Kickstart → The Foundation

**I have no time →** The Executive

**I'm burned out / coming back from injury →** The Reset

**I want to focus on skin →** The Skin Protocol

**I travel constantly →** The Executive

**I want the full transformation →** The Foundation → ongoing maintenance

Multiple programs can be run simultaneously. For example, The Executive (fitness) + The Skin Protocol (skincare) is a common stack for busy professionals.

chunks.title: skincare — Philosophy
chunks.content: Skincare at HIRO is about healthy, confident skin at any age. The focus is on effective routines that work with your lifestyle, not against it. No 12-step routines. No fear-based marketing. Just what actually works.

chunks.title: skincare — Consistency Over Complexity
chunks.content:
- A simple routine done daily beats a complex one done occasionally
- 3-4 products used consistently is the sweet spot
- Results take 4-12 weeks — patience is essential

chunks.title: skincare — Sun Protection is Non-Negotiable
chunks.content:
- SPF 30+ every single day, regardless of weather
- UV exposure is responsible for 80% of visible skin aging
- Reapply every 2 hours when outdoors
- This is the single highest-impact skincare decision you can make

chunks.title: skincare — Hydration From Inside and Out
chunks.content:
- Water intake affects skin hydration
- Moisturizer seals in hydration
- Humidifier in dry environments helps significantly

chunks.title: skincare — Morning
chunks.content:
1. Cleanse (gentle, non-stripping)
2. Vitamin C serum (antioxidant protection)
3. Moisturizer
4. SPF 30+

chunks.title: skincare — Evening
chunks.content:
1. Double cleanse (oil cleanser + gentle foaming cleanser)
2. Treatment (retinoid or exfoliant — not both same night)
3. Moisturizer

chunks.title: skincare — Anti-Aging
chunks.content:
- Retinoids (retinol, retinaldehyde, tretinoin): gold standard, start slow
- Peptides: support collagen production
- Vitamin C: antioxidant, brightens, supports collagen
- Niacinamide: improves skin barrier, reduces fine lines

chunks.title: skincare — Hyperpigmentation / Dark Spots
chunks.content:
- Vitamin C: brightens and prevents
- Azelaic acid: gentle, effective for most skin types
- Alpha arbutin: targets pigment production
- Tranexamic acid: effective for stubborn spots
- SPF: non-negotiable — treatments are useless without it

chunks.title: skincare — Acne / Breakouts (yes, after 30 too)
chunks.content:
- Salicylic acid (BHA): unclogs pores, 2-3x/week
- Benzoyl peroxide: kills acne bacteria, use as spot treatment
- Retinoids: regulate cell turnover long-term
- Niacinamide: reduces inflammation

chunks.title: skincare — Dryness / Dehydration
chunks.content:
- Hyaluronic acid: apply to damp skin
- Ceramides: repair skin barrier
- Squalane: lightweight, effective moisturizer
- Avoid hot water when washing face

chunks.title: skincare — Age-Specific Notes (30+)
chunks.content:
- Cell turnover slows — gentle exfoliation becomes more important
- Collagen production declines — retinoids and peptides help
- Hormonal changes can trigger adult acne
- Skin becomes drier — adjust moisturizer weight seasonally
- Eye area shows age first — dedicated eye cream can help

chunks.title: skincare — Sleep
chunks.content:
- "Beauty sleep" is real — skin repairs at night
- 7-8 hours for optimal skin regeneration
- Sleep on your back to prevent sleep lines

chunks.title: skincare — Nutrition for Skin
chunks.content:
- Omega-3s reduce inflammation
- Vitamin C-rich foods support collagen
- Antioxidants (berries, green tea, dark chocolate) fight free radicals
- Limit sugar — it accelerates glycation (skin aging)
- Moderate alcohol — it dehydrates and inflames

chunks.title: skincare — Stress
chunks.content:
- Cortisol triggers breakouts and accelerates aging
- Stress management is skincare
- Exercise improves circulation to skin

chunks.title: skincare — Common Mistakes
chunks.content:
- Starting too many actives at once (irritation, barrier damage)
- Skipping SPF when using actives (increased sun sensitivity)
- Over-exfoliating (compromised barrier)
- Expecting overnight results
- Using products meant for 20-year-old skin
